findings.title: Pre-fill BAC Water in Reconstitution Calculator
findings.current_behavior: The reconstitution calculator requires manual entry for vial size, BAC water volume, and target dose every time.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When calculating reconstitution for a compound, pre-fill the 'BAC Water Volume' and 'Vial Size' fields with the values from the user's most recent reconstitution of that same compound. Include a small 'use last' button for one-tap recall.
findings.user_benefit: Speeds up the safety-critical reconstitution flow for Power Users who use consistent concentrations, reducing repetitive entry and risk of manual error.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires a simple query for the user's last 'VialReconstituted' event for the compound. Frontend logic is minimal.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I previously reconstituted 5mg of BPC-157 with 2mL of BAC water, when I open the calculator for BPC-157 again, then the 'Vial Size' is '5mg' and 'BAC Water Volume' is '2mL'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A smart default that reduces effort on an existing feature.

findings.title: Visual Syringe Preview in Reconstitution
findings.current_behavior: The calculator shows the dose in syringe units as text (e.g., '10 units').
findings.proposed_enhancement: Alongside the text, display a simple, clear graphical representation of a 1mL insulin syringe with the plunger drawn to the calculated number of units. Highlight the measurement.
findings.user_benefit: Provides an immediate, unambiguous visual confirmation of the dose volume, drastically reducing the risk of misreading text and making a catastrophic dosing error. This is a massive safety and trust builder.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires creating a reusable SVG or component that can dynamically render the syringe based on an input value. Not complex, but requires design and frontend effort.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given the calculator determines a dose of 250mcg is '10 units', when the result is displayed, then I see a graphic of a syringe with the plunger at the 10-unit mark.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A 'wow moment' and UX refinement that enhances a safety-critical flow without adding a new feature.

findings.title: Visual Diff for Crypto Wallet Confirmation
findings.current_behavior: The user must manually compare the vendor's wallet address with the one from their last order, then re-enter it to enable the confirmation button.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When the user pastes the new wallet address, the UI performs a character-by-character 'diff' against the previously used address for that vendor. Highlight added, removed, or changed characters in red/green, making any change instantly obvious.
findings.user_benefit: Protects the Power User from non-reversible crypto payment errors caused by vendor address changes or clipboard-hijacking malware. Turns a stressful manual check into a confident, quick verification.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires a frontend component to perform the diff and render the highlighted text. Logic is moderately complex but self-contained.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given my last order used address 'bc1...xyz' and I paste 'bc1...abc', when the verification UI is shown, then the differing characters 'abc' are highlighted in green and 'xyz' in red.
findings.scope_check: In scope. An inline validation and error handling improvement for a safety-critical flow.

findings.title: 5-Second Undo for Dose Logging
findings.current_behavior: Logging a dose is an immediate, final action. A mistake requires manually finding and deleting the log entry.
findings.proposed_enhancement: After a user confirms a dose log (individual or batch), display a temporary toast notification for 5-7 seconds with an 'Undo' button. Tapping it immediately reverts the logging action.
findings.user_benefit: Provides a frictionless way to correct common mis-taps, especially for the Power User in their 3-minute morning window. This is a standard, expected UX pattern that builds user confidence.
findings.cost_reasoning: The 'log' action can be delayed by 7 seconds, or more robustly, an inverse 'un-log' action can be triggered. Most UI libraries have toast components.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I have just logged a dose, when the confirmation appears, then I see an 'Undo' button for 7 seconds. When I tap 'Undo', then the dose log is deleted and the dose returns to 'pending' status.
findings.scope_check: In scope. Adds undo/redo to an existing flow.

findings.title: Visual Injection Site Heatmap
findings.current_behavior: The app suggests the next injection site in a rotation and shows the last 7 sites as a text list.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Instead of a list, display a simple anatomical diagram (e.g., abdomen, thighs). Overlay a 'heatmap' where the most recently used sites for that compound glow brightly, fading over time. The suggested site could pulse.
findings.user_benefit: Provides an intuitive, at-a-glance understanding of site rotation history, which is far superior to reading a list. Helps users make better decisions to avoid tissue trauma.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires an SVG diagram and frontend logic to map site data to coordinates and apply dynamic styles based on timestamps.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am logging a dose, when the site selection UI appears, then I see a diagram of a body where my last injection site 'left abdomen' is highlighted in a bright color, and older sites are progressively dimmer.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A 'wow moment' that enhances an existing feature without adding new functionality.

findings.title: Persistent Offline Status Banner
findings.current_behavior: Offline logging is queued, but the user has no persistent visual feedback that they are in offline mode.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Display a slim, non-intrusive banner at the top or bottom of the screen whenever the PWA detects it is offline ('Offline mode: Logs are queued'). Upon reconnection, it changes to 'Syncing...' and then 'Synced' before disappearing.
findings.user_benefit: Gives both Power Users and Delegated Participants confidence that their actions are being saved, and informs them why some data might not be up-to-date. Removes ambiguity and anxiety about data loss.
findings.cost_reasoning: Leverages standard PWA service worker and browser 'navigator.onLine' events. Most UI frameworks have a banner/alert component.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am offline and open the app, when the app loads, then I see a persistent banner stating 'Offline mode'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A critical refinement for mobile/offline UX.

findings.title: Real-time Syringe Unit Conversion in Protocol Editor
findings.current_behavior: Users define a dose in mcg/mg in the protocol, but only see the syringe unit conversion in the separate reconstitution calculator.
findings.proposed_enhancement: In the protocol creation/edit screen, as the user enters the dose in mcg, show the equivalent in 'syringe units' in real-time below the input field. This requires the user to have a reconstituted vial for that compound.
findings.user_benefit: Connects the abstract protocol dose with the physical action of drawing it. Helps a Power User sanity-check their numbers and build intuition without context-switching to the calculator.
findings.cost_reasoning: Reuses the exact same logic from US-REC-01. Requires a query for the active vial's concentration for that user/compound.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I have a vial of BPC-157 reconstituted at 2500mcg/mL, when I edit a protocol for BPC-157 and type '250' in the dose field, then helper text appears saying '10 units'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. Inline validation that leverages existing data.

findings.title: Guided First Protocol Creation for New Power Users
findings.current_behavior: The dashboard shows a summary, which would be empty for a new user.
findings.proposed_enhancement: For a new user with no active protocols, the dashboard should display a welcoming empty state component. It should guide them with a large, clear Call to Action: 'Your dashboard is ready. Let's create your first protocol to get started.'
findings.user_benefit: Orients new Power Users and immediately directs them to the primary value-creating action. Prevents confusion or the feeling that the app is broken on first login.
findings.cost_reasoning: Simple conditional rendering in the dashboard component based on whether `protocols.length > 0`.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am a new user who has just completed registration, when I land on the dashboard, then I see a component with a headline 'Welcome!' and a primary button 'Create Your First Protocol'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A critical empty-state design for the main dashboard.

findings.title: Contextual Protocol Defaults
findings.current_behavior: Creating a new protocol starts with empty fields for dose and frequency.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When a Power User creates a protocol for a compound they've used before, pre-fill the 'dose' and 'frequency' fields with the values from their most recent protocol for that same compound.
findings.user_benefit: Saves time and reduces cognitive load for Power Users who are restarting a cycle or creating a slightly modified version of a previous protocol. Caters directly to the 'speed and control' persona need.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires a simple query for the user's most recent protocol for the selected compound. Minimal frontend logic.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given my last BPC-157 protocol was 250mcg daily, when I create a new protocol and select 'BPC-157', then the dose field is pre-filled with '250mcg' and the frequency is set to 'daily'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A smart default for an existing feature.

findings.title: Reason for Inventory-Aware Order Suggestions
findings.current_behavior: The order builder lists suggested compounds based on low inventory.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Next to each suggested compound, provide the specific reason it's being suggested. For example: 'BPC-157 (8 days remaining at current rate)' or 'Ipamorelin (Vial expires in 3 days)'.
findings.user_benefit: Provides crucial context that helps the Power User prioritize their order. It answers the 'why' and builds trust that the suggestions are intelligent and based on real data.
findings.cost_reasoning: The data to calculate 'days remaining' is already being used to generate the suggestion itself. This is just about displaying it in the UI.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given BPC-157 supply is projected to last 8 more days, when I open the order builder, then I see 'BPC-157' in the suggested list with the text '(8 days remaining)'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. Progressive disclosure within an existing interface.

findings.title: Large Dose Deviation Confirmation
findings.current_behavior: A user can log any dose amount, even one that's wildly different from the scheduled amount, without any warning.
findings.proposed_enhancement: When logging a dose, if the user manually changes the amount to a value that deviates by more than a configurable threshold (e.g., +/- 50%) from the scheduled protocol amount, show a confirmation modal: 'This dose (1000mcg) is much larger than your scheduled dose (250mcg). Are you sure?'
findings.user_benefit: Acts as a critical safety net to prevent potentially dangerous typos (e.g., adding an extra zero). It respects user autonomy but prevents high-risk mistakes.
findings.cost_reasoning: Simple conditional logic in the dose logging component.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given my scheduled dose is 250mcg, when I attempt to log a manual dose of 2500mcg, then a confirmation dialog appears asking me to verify the amount before proceeding.
findings.scope_check: In scope. Inline validation and error handling for an existing flow.

findings.title: Batch 'Add All Suggested' to Order
findings.current_behavior: Users must add each suggested item to their cart one by one.
findings.proposed_enhancement: In the 'Suggested' section of the order builder, provide a single 'Add All to Cart' button that adds all the low-inventory compounds to the order simultaneously.
findings.user_benefit: Saves multiple taps for the Power User who trusts the suggestions and wants to quickly replenish all their low supplies. A simple speed and efficiency win.
findings.cost_reasoning: A simple loop on the frontend to trigger the 'add to cart' action for each item in the suggestions array.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given there are 3 items in the 'Suggested' section, when I tap the 'Add All to Cart' button, then all 3 items appear in my cart.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A batch operation within an existing feature.

findings.title: AI-Generated Plain-Language Compound Summary
findings.current_behavior: The compound profile shows a technical 'mechanism of action'.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On the compound profile page, include a toggle or separate section labeled 'Simple Explanation'. Use a fine-tuned LLM to generate a one-paragraph, easy-to-understand summary of the peptide's purpose and mechanism, based on the technical source text. This would be generated once and stored, not on-the-fly.
findings.user_benefit: Makes the reference pillar accessible to the 'Delegated Participant' persona, who would be lost by the technical jargon. Helps them understand *why* they are taking something, increasing adherence and comfort.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires an offline script or backend process to run content through an LLM API upon profile creation/update. The cost is per-generation, not per-view. Requires prompt engineering to ensure accuracy and tone.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am viewing the BPC-157 profile, when I tap 'Simple Explanation', then I see text like 'This peptide is thought to help the body's natural healing processes, like building new blood vessels, which can speed up recovery from injury.'
findings.scope_check: In scope. An AI-native enhancement that fits within an existing story (US-REF-01) and does not provide recommendations.

findings.title: Haptic Feedback on Critical Actions
findings.current_behavior: Button taps provide no physical feedback.
findings.proposed_enhancement: On mobile (PWA), provide subtle haptic feedback for primary confirmation actions, such as 'Confirm' on a dose log, 'Confirm All' on a batch log, and especially the final 'Mark Payment Sent' button. A success buzz for confirmation, a slight vibration for an error.
findings.user_benefit: Improves the mobile-first experience and accessibility by providing non-visual confirmation that a critical action was registered. It makes the app feel more responsive and 'real'.
findings.cost_reasoning: The browser Vibration API (`navigator.vibrate`) is a single line of code, wrapped in a check for support.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am on a mobile device, when I tap the 'Confirm' button to log a dose, then my device vibrates briefly.
findings.scope_check: In scope. An accessibility and 'wow moment' micro-interaction.

findings.title: Show Active Protocols When Assigning to Managed User
findings.current_behavior: A Power User can assign a protocol to a managed user without seeing what else that user is already on.
findings.proposed_enhancement: In the 'Assign to User' dropdown or modal within the protocol editor, if a managed user is selected, dynamically display a small list of that user's currently active protocols directly in the UI.
findings.user_benefit: Helps the Power User avoid accidentally creating conflicting or overlapping protocols for their managed users. Provides just-in-time context to make a better decision.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires a simple data fetch for the selected user's active protocols when the selection changes. Low frontend effort.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given a managed user is on an active 'BPC-157' protocol, when I create a new protocol and select that user from the 'Assign to' list, then I see 'Also on: BPC-157' displayed below their name.
findings.scope_check: In scope. Progressive disclosure within an existing interface.

findings.title: Clone Protocol to New Managed User
findings.current_behavior: After creating a new managed user, the Power User must manually create new protocols for them from scratch.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Upon successful creation of a new Managed User, present an optional next step: 'Set up their protocols?' with an option to 'Clone an existing protocol'. This flow would let the Power User select one of their own or another user's protocols to clone directly to the new user.
findings.user_benefit: Streamlines the onboarding of a family member, which is a key Power User task. Reduces repetitive data entry when starting a new person on a standard, established protocol.
findings.cost_reasoning: Combines the UI flows for user creation and protocol cloning, requiring some new modal/view logic. The backend action is already defined in US-TRK-02.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I have just created a new managed user, when the success message appears, then I see a button 'Clone Protocol for [New User]'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A smart default/workflow enhancement that combines existing features.

findings.title: Home Screen Icon Badge for Pending Doses
findings.current_behavior: The user must open the app to see what they need to do.
findings.proposed_enhancement: Use the PWA Badging API to display the number of pending doses for the day on the app's icon on the user's home screen. The badge would appear when the first reminder is sent and clear when all doses are logged or skipped.
findings.user_benefit: Provides an ambient, at-a-glance status check without even opening the app. Serves as a subtle, persistent reminder for both user personas and reinforces the app's role as a daily tool.
findings.cost_reasoning: Requires service worker logic to calculate the daily dose count and use the Badging API. Involves coordinating background sync and state.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I have 3 doses scheduled for today and have not logged them, when I look at my phone's home screen, then the app icon has a badge showing the number '3'.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A 'wow moment' and mobile/PWA refinement.

findings.title: Keyboard Shortcut for 'Log All Scheduled'
findings.proposed_enhancement: For PWA usage on a desktop or with a connected keyboard, implement a keyboard shortcut (e.g., `Cmd+L` or `Cmd+Enter`) on the dashboard that triggers the 'Log All Scheduled' action.
findings.user_benefit: Caters directly to the Power User's need for speed and efficiency. Allows them to complete their primary morning task in sub-second time without taking their hands off the keyboard.
findings.cost_reasoning: Adding a global keydown event listener is a very small amount of frontend code.
findings.acceptance_criteria_addition: Given I am on the dashboard and have pending doses, when I press 'Cmd+Enter', then the 'Log All Scheduled' confirmation dialog appears.
findings.scope_check: In scope. A keyboard shortcut for an existing feature.
